nodes.name: openspec-apply-change (Claude Code)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Implement tasks from an OpenSpec change. Covers task discovery, implementation workflow, and post-execution hooks for the Claude Code agent.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Defines agent behavior for the Claude Code environment via openspec CLI integration.

nodes.name: openspec-archive-change (Claude Code)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Archive a completed OpenSpec change. Finalizes and archives changes after implementation is complete.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Provides step-by-step archiving workflow for the Claude Code agent.

nodes.name: openspec-explore (Claude Code)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Explore mode for OpenSpec — a thinking partner for exploring ideas, investigating problems, and clarifying requirements. Read-only stance with no implementation.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Largest OpenSpec skill file with 10 sections covering stance, discovery, and guardrails. Defines the explore mode as a stancenot a workflow.

nodes.name: openspec-propose (Claude Code)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Propose a new OpenSpec change with all artifacts generated in one step — design, specs, and tasks ready for implementation.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Enables rapid change proposal generation for the Claude Code agent.

nodes.name: openspec-sync-specs (Claude Code)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Sync delta specs from an OpenSpec change to main specs without archiving the change. Updates main specs with changes from delta specs.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Handles spec synchronization workflow between delta and main specs.

nodes.name: speckit-agent-context-update (Claude Code)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Refresh the managed Spec Kit section in the coding agent context file, ensuring project context stays current.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Small file (31 lines) defining context update behavior for the Claude Code agent.

nodes.name: speckit-analyze (Claude Code)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Perform cross-artifact consistency and quality analysis across spec.md, plan.md, and tasks.md after task generation. Non-destructive analysis.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. 260 lines with 25 sections covering 9 detection passes and severity assignment.

nodes.name: speckit-checklist (Claude Code)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Generate a custom checklist for the current feature based on user requirements. Described as 'Unit Tests for English'.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. 374 lines with 7 sections including anti-examples of what NOT to do.

nodes.name: speckit-clarify (Claude Code)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Identify underspecified areas in the current feature spec by asking up to 5 highly targeted clarification questions and encoding answers back into the spec.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. 286 lines with 6 sections including mandatory post-execution hooks.

nodes.name: speckit-constitution (Claude Code)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Create or update the project constitution from interactive or provided principle inputs, ensuring all dependent templates stay in sync.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. 157 lines defining project constitution creation and template synchronization.

nodes.name: speckit-implement (Claude Code)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Execute the implementation plan by processing and executing all tasks defined in tasks.md with mandatory post-execution hooks.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. 224 lines with task-by-task implementation workflow and completion reporting.

nodes.name: speckit-plan (Claude Code)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Execute the implementation planning workflow using the plan template to generate design artifacts across Phase 0 (Research) and Phase 1 (Design & Contracts).
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. 171 lines with 10 sections covering two-phase planning workflow.

nodes.name: speckit-specify (Claude Code)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Create or update the feature specification from a natural language feature description, with section requirements and success criteria guidelines.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. 346 lines with 10 sections — the second-largest SpecKit skill. Includes AI generation guidance.

nodes.name: speckit-tasks (Claude Code)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Generate an actionable, dependency-ordered tasks.md for the feature based on available design artifacts. Includes checklist format and phase structure rules.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. 215 lines with 10 sections covering task generation rules and organization.

nodes.name: speckit-taskstoissues (Claude Code)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Convert existing tasks into actionable, dependency-ordered GitHub issues for the feature based on available design artifacts.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. 107 lines with GitHub issue export workflow.

nodes.name: copilot-instructions
nodes.summary: GitHub Copilot instructions pointing to Speckit context. Directs Copilot to read the current plan for project structure, shell commands, and technology context.
nodes.languageNotes: Small Markdown file (4 lines) with HTML comment markers for Speckit START/END blocks.

nodes.name: openspec-apply-change (GitHub Copilot)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Implement tasks from an OpenSpec change. Identical to the Claude Code version, deployed for GitHub Copilot agent environment.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Duplicate of the Claude Code skill file for GitHub Copilot compatibility.

nodes.name: openspec-archive-change (GitHub Copilot)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Archive a completed OpenSpec change. Duplicate of the Claude Code version for GitHub Copilot.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Duplicate of the Claude Code skill file.

nodes.name: openspec-explore (GitHub Copilot)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Explore mode for OpenSpec. Duplicate of the Claude Code version for GitHub Copilot agent. Read-only thinking partner stance.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Identical 10-section structure as the Claude Code version.

nodes.name: openspec-propose (GitHub Copilot)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Propose a new OpenSpec change. Duplicate of the Claude Code version for GitHub Copilot agent.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Duplicate of the Claude Code skill file.

nodes.name: openspec-sync-specs (GitHub Copilot)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Sync delta specs. Duplicate of the Claude Code version for GitHub Copilot agent.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Duplicate of the Claude Code skill file.

nodes.name: openspec-apply-change (OpenCode)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Implement tasks from an OpenSpec change. Duplicate deployed for OpenCode agent environment.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Duplicate of the Claude Code skill file for OpenCode compatibility.

nodes.name: openspec-archive-change (OpenCode)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Archive a completed OpenSpec change. Duplicate for OpenCode agent environment.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Duplicate of the Claude Code skill file.

nodes.name: openspec-explore (OpenCode)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Explore mode for OpenSpec. Duplicate for OpenCode agent environment.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Identical 10-section structure as the Claude Code version.

nodes.name: openspec-propose (OpenCode)
nodes.summary: AI skill: Propose a new OpenSpec change. Duplicate for OpenCode agent environment.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Duplicate of the Claude Code skill file.

nodes.name: OpenSpec Workflow
nodes.summary: An AI-driven spec-driven development workflow consisting of 5 phases: explore (investigate/think), propose (create change proposal with design/specs/tasks), apply (implement tasks), sync-specs (update main specs from delta), and archive (finalize completed changes). Requires openspec CLI.
nodes.languageNotes: The OpenSpec workflow is deployed across three AI agent environments: Claude Code (.claude/skills/), GitHub Copilot (.github/skills/), and OpenCode (.opencode/skills/). Each skill file is an identical Markdown document with YAML frontmatter.

nodes.name: OpenSpec Apply Change
nodes.summary: Implements tasks from an OpenSpec change proposal. Discovers tasks, executes them in dependency order, and reports completion status with post-execution hooks.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of the OpenSpec workflow. Available in 3 AI agent environments.

nodes.name: OpenSpec Archive Change
nodes.summary: Finalizes and archives a completed OpenSpec change, moving it from active to archived state with all artifacts preserved.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of the OpenSpec workflow. Available in 3 AI agent environments.

nodes.name: OpenSpec Explore
nodes.summary: A thinking partner mode for exploring ideas, investigating problems, and clarifying requirements. Read-only stance — no implementation allowed. May create OpenSpec artifacts (proposals, designs, specs) as captured thinking.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of the OpenSpec workflow. Largest skill file (287 lines). Available in 3 AI agent environments.

nodes.name: OpenSpec Propose
nodes.summary: Generates a complete change proposal in one step — design document, feature specs, and implementation tasks — from a natural language description of what to build.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of the OpenSpec workflow. Available in 3 AI agent environments.

nodes.name: OpenSpec Sync Specs
nodes.summary: Synchronizes delta specs from an OpenSpec change into the main specification files without archiving the change. Allows iterative spec updates during implementation.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of the OpenSpec workflow. Available in 3 AI agent environments.

nodes.name: SpecKit Workflow
nodes.summary: A structured specification and implementation workflow (Spec Kit) consisting of 10 phases: constitution (project governance), specify (feature spec), clarify (identify gaps), plan (design artifacts), tasks (dependency-ordered task list), taskstoissues (GitHub issue export), analyze (cross-artifact quality check), checklist (quality checklist), implement (execute tasks), and agent-context-update (refresh coding agent context). Requires spec-kit project structure with .specify/ directory.
nodes.languageNotes: All Speckit skills are authored by github-spec-kit and deployed in the Claude Code environment (.claude/skills/). They form a complete specification-to-implementation pipeline.

nodes.name: SpecKit Agent Context Update
nodes.summary: Refreshes the managed Spec Kit section in the coding agent's context file to keep project structure and technology information current for the AI agent.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of the SpecKit workflow. Smallest skill file (31 lines).

nodes.name: SpecKit Analyze
nodes.summary: Performs non-destructive cross-artifact consistency and quality analysis across spec.md, plan.md, and tasks.md. Runs 9 detection passes: duplication, ambiguity, underspecification, constitution alignment, coverage gaps, inconsistency, plus severity assignment and remediation suggestions.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of the SpecKit workflow. 260 lines with 25 sections covering comprehensive analysis.

nodes.name: SpecKit Checklist
nodes.summary: Generates a custom quality checklist for the current feature based on user requirements. Described as 'Unit Tests for English' — validates specification completeness through structured checklist items.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of the SpecKit workflow. Largest SpecKit skill (374 lines) with anti-example guidance.

nodes.name: SpecKit Clarify
nodes.summary: Identifies underspecified areas in the current feature spec by asking up to 5 highly targeted clarification questions and encoding the answers back into the specification.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of the SpecKit workflow. Includes mandatory post-execution hooks for spec updates.

nodes.name: SpecKit Constitution
nodes.summary: Creates or updates the project constitution from interactive or provided principle inputs, ensuring all dependent templates stay in sync with governance rules.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of the SpecKit workflow. Defines project-level governance and template synchronization.

nodes.name: SpecKit Implement
nodes.summary: Executes the implementation plan by processing all tasks defined in tasks.md in dependency order. Includes mandatory post-execution hooks and completion reporting.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of the SpecKit workflow. The execution phase of the specification pipeline.

nodes.name: SpecKit Plan
nodes.summary: Executes the implementation planning workflow using the plan template. Two phases: Phase 0 (Outline & Research) and Phase 1 (Design & Contracts), generating design artifacts.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of the SpecKit workflow. Bridges specification and implementation through structured planning.

nodes.name: SpecKit Specify
nodes.summary: Creates or updates the feature specification from a natural language feature description. Includes section requirements, AI generation guidance, and success criteria guidelines.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of the SpecKit workflow. Second-largest SpecKit skill (346 lines) with detailed AI generation rules.

nodes.name: SpecKit Tasks
nodes.summary: Generates an actionable, dependency-ordered tasks.md for the feature based on available design artifacts. Includes checklist format requirements and phase structure rules.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of the SpecKit workflow. Transforms specifications into implementable task lists.

nodes.name: SpecKit Tasks to Issues
nodes.summary: Converts existing tasks from tasks.md into actionable, dependency-ordered GitHub issues with proper labels and cross-references.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of the SpecKit workflow. Bridges task management and GitHub issue tracking.
